visa_types.documents_required:
The Customers are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
- A filled in visa application (all columns need to be filled in with blue ink). The application shall be filled in completely and legibly in block letters. Names shall mandatorily be spelled in Latin letters (as spelled in the international passport). The rest of the information shall be completed in Russian and English. The application shall be signed by the Customer in person; In the form of minors children, BOTH parents must be subscribe their sign. If there is a signature from only the one parent, they must provide a notarized power of attorney for the right to sign and submit.
- A foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid for at least six months after the end of the intended trip (for a multiple-entry visa – after the end of the last intended trip), containing at least two blank pages.
- A photocopy of the first page of international passport and a photocopy of the latest Bulgarian and Schengen visas if any.
- A 3.5 by 4.5 cm color photograph with a light background. Face one and the face in it shall be clear and large, occupying 70 to 80% of the photograph. No red, eye effect shall be visible. Photographs in which the person wears darkened glasses, art (unprofessional) photographs, or images cropped from amateur photographs WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.
- Travel health insurance to cover the possible expenses related to repatriation, for health reasons, urgent medical assistance and/or urgent hospitalization, with the minimum coverage of EUR 30 000. The insurance must be valid for the territory of the Republic of Bulgaria during the entire stay in Bulgaria.
- Proof of residence for nationals other than Kazakhstan: a photocopy of the stay permit; Citizens of Kyrgyzstan, Turkmenistan, and Tajikistan don’t need be a residence of Kazakhstan for submitting documents for visa (they do not have a Consulate).
- Employee letter on official letterhead with stamp, address, phone number, position and salary of person travelling.
In case all expenses are covered by the employer, it is necessary to provide a business trip letter indicating the purpose and duration of the applicant’s trip. 
- Invitation letter with signature and stamp Travel order from the relevant Employer authorizing the trip.
- Means of transportation and intention to return to the country of residence: reservation of return travel ticket for airplane, but, If the person is travelling to a third country, then proof of itinerary (travel ticket, etc.).
- Proof of family ties (copies of marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.)
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR TRAVELING WITH MINORS

Submission of a visa application for minors:
- Young children (under 14) or minors under guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Minors (aged 14 to 18) or persons under limited guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the minor and also by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Parental presence, signature, or consent is not required if the parent: 
- has been deprived of parental rights – an apostilled notarized copy of the final court decision must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is deceased – an apostilled notarized copy of the parent’s death certificate must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is absent (if the birth certificate lists as “father” a close person or someone who is not the legal parent) – the original document issued by the competent state authority confirming that the father’s name was entered in the birth certificate based on the mother’s statement must be provided.
- Documents confirming the father’s absence are not required only if the birth certificate shows a dash in the “father” field. In this case, an apostilled notarized copy of the birth certificate must be included in the document set.
- If the child has no parents but has an official guardian, the corresponding document establishing guardianship must be provided. An apostilled notarized copy of the guardianship document with a translation into Bulgarian must be included.
- For minors traveling without their parents/guardians and requesting a single/multiple-entry visa, a notarized copy of the notarized consent/authorization (either for travel with another person or unaccompanied) from both parents/guardians or from the parent/guardian not accompanying the minor must be provided for the entire duration of the requested visa.
- In all cases, a copy of the birth certificate is required for minors, as well as a separate set of visa application documents, even if the minor is included in a parent’s passport.
Applications for minor children must be submitted:
- On the same day together with the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip; or
- Not earlier than the application of the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip is under review at the consulate; or
- If the accompanying adult holds a valid Schengen visa for the entire duration of the trip.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Citizens of Kazakhstan who plan to visit Bulgaria are required to have a visa.
IMPORTANT NOTICE
Citizens of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan don’t need be a residence of Kazakhstan for submitting documents for visa (they do not have an Embassy).
A short-stay visa (C-visa) allows the visitor to transit through or stay in the territory of the Republic of Bulgaria for a maximum of 90 days in any period 180 day-period. It is issued for one, two, or multiple entries for the purpose of transit, tourism, business, private or other purposes. Its validity period depends on the circumstances of the visitor’s travel to Bulgaria and may not exceed five years.
Customers cannot work in Bulgaria by short term visa. 
Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued.
An issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Bulgaria. On every crossing of the border, the police may require the foreign citizen
                                                                        to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits. 
The decision to refuse a visa, indicating reasons for such a decision, shall be delivered to the Customer in a visa refusal/annulment/revocation form.
A foreign citizen can file a complaint against a decision to refuse to issue a visa at any time suitable for him. 
The visa application fee is non-refundable once the application has been accepted at the Embassy.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution

## Overview
Citizens of Kazakhstan who plan to visit Bulgaria are required to have a visa.
IMPORTANT NOTICE
Citizens of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan don’t need be a residence of Kazakhstan for submitting documents for visa (they do not have an Embassy).
A short-stay visa (C-visa) allows the visitor to transit through or stay in the territory of the Republic of Bulgaria for a maximum of 90 days in any period 180 day-period. It is issued for one, two, or multiple entries for the purpose of transit, tourism, business, private or other purposes. Its validity period depends on the circumstances of the visitor’s travel to Bulgaria and may not exceed five years.
Customers cannot work in Bulgaria by short term visa. 
Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued.
An issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Bulgaria. On every crossing of the border, the police may require the foreign citizen
                                                                        to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits. 
The decision to refuse a visa, indicating reasons for such a decision, shall be delivered to the Customer in a visa refusal/annulment/revocation form.
A foreign citizen can file a complaint against a decision to refuse to issue a visa at any time suitable for him. 
The visa application fee is non-refundable once the application has been accepted at the Embassy.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution

## Visa Fees

[Table:Official]
Please Note: 
There is a service charge of 17 565 KZT (Inclusive of VAT 16%) levied per application over and above the visa fees
Note: All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable. 
Please note, that all payments for visa and service fee accepted in local currency (KZT).
                                                                    

## Documents Required
The Customers are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
- A filled in visa application (all columns need to be filled in with blue ink). The application shall be filled in completely and legibly in block letters. Names shall mandatorily be spelled in Latin letters (as spelled in the international passport). The rest of the information shall be completed in Russian and English. The application shall be signed by the Customer in person; In the form of minors children, BOTH parents must be subscribe their sign. If there is a signature from only the one parent, they must provide a notarized power of attorney for the right to sign and submit.
- A foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid for at least six months after the end of the intended trip (for a multiple-entry visa – after the end of the last intended trip), containing at least two blank pages.
- A photocopy of the first page of international passport and a photocopy of the latest Bulgarian and Schengen visas if any.
- A 3.5 by 4.5 cm color photograph with a light background. Face one and the face in it shall be clear and large, occupying 70 to 80% of the photograph. No red, eye effect shall be visible. Photographs in which the person wears darkened glasses, art (unprofessional) photographs, or images cropped from amateur photographs WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.
- Travel health insurance to cover the possible expenses related to repatriation, for health reasons, urgent medical assistance and/or urgent hospitalization, with the minimum coverage of EUR 30 000. The insurance must be valid for the territory of the Republic of Bulgaria during the entire stay in Bulgaria.
- Proof of residence for nationals other than Kazakhstan: a photocopy of the stay permit; Citizens of Kyrgyzstan, Turkmenistan, and Tajikistan don’t need be a residence of Kazakhstan for submitting documents for visa (they do not have a Consulate).
- Employee letter on official letterhead with stamp, address, phone number, position and salary of person travelling.
In case all expenses are covered by the employer, it is necessary to provide a business trip letter indicating the purpose and duration of the applicant’s trip. 
- Invitation letter with signature and stamp Travel order from the relevant Employer authorizing the trip.
- Means of transportation and intention to return to the country of residence: reservation of return travel ticket for airplane, but, If the person is travelling to a third country, then proof of itinerary (travel ticket, etc.).
- Proof of family ties (copies of marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.)
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR TRAVELING WITH MINORS

Submission of a visa application for minors:
- Young children (under 14) or minors under guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Minors (aged 14 to 18) or persons under limited guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the minor and also by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Parental presence, signature, or consent is not required if the parent: 
- has been deprived of parental rights – an apostilled notarized copy of the final court decision must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is deceased – an apostilled notarized copy of the parent’s death certificate must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is absent (if the birth certificate lists as “father” a close person or someone who is not the legal parent) – the original document issued by the competent state authority confirming that the father’s name was entered in the birth certificate based on the mother’s statement must be provided.
- Documents confirming the father’s absence are not required only if the birth certificate shows a dash in the “father” field. In this case, an apostilled notarized copy of the birth certificate must be included in the document set.
- If the child has no parents but has an official guardian, the corresponding document establishing guardianship must be provided. An apostilled notarized copy of the guardianship document with a translation into Bulgarian must be included.
- For minors traveling without their parents/guardians and requesting a single/multiple-entry visa, a notarized copy of the notarized consent/authorization (either for travel with another person or unaccompanied) from both parents/guardians or from the parent/guardian not accompanying the minor must be provided for the entire duration of the requested visa.
- In all cases, a copy of the birth certificate is required for minors, as well as a separate set of visa application documents, even if the minor is included in a parent’s passport.
Applications for minor children must be submitted:
- On the same day together with the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip; or
- Not earlier than the application of the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip is under review at the consulate; or
- If the accompanying adult holds a valid Schengen visa for the entire duration of the trip.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa Customer must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last 6 months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa Customer submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa Customer is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the Customer's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The average visa processing time is 15 calendar days after the application is accepted at the embassy (processing may be faster, depending on the documents). In some cases, if additional consideration is necessary, this period can be extended to a maximum of 45-60 calendar days.

## Download Forms
Dear Customers, please note that all Bulgaria visa application centers offer assisted form filling service. 
To download a document required please choose one of the links below:
Visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3V3eQu0QUCGhOP6jd6Ci7p/2f78c24ee2ae23cf4b8e1f6ed653b811/visa_application_form_latest.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.
Please note:
- Visa application form should be on 3 pages.
- It should be printed both sides.
You can also get preprinted application form in the Visa Application Center in Astana and Almaty.

- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm

- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa Customer is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the Customer's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Citizens of Kazakhstan who plan to visit Bulgaria are required to have a visa.
The documents below need to be submitted in addition to the Mandatory documents for all visa categories. 
Invitation letter from the inviting organization/party with signature and stamp; 
Traveling instruction such as leave letter from Kazakh organization; 
Employment letter on official letterhead with stamp, address, phone number, position and salary;
IMPORTANT NOTICE
Citizens of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan don’t need be a residence of Kazakhstan for submitting documents for visa (they do not have an Embassy).
A short-stay visa (C-visa) allows the visitor to transit through or stay in the territory of the Republic of Bulgaria for a maximum of 90 days in any period 180 day-period. It is issued for one, two, or multiple entries for the purpose of transit, tourism, business, private or other purposes. Its validity period depends on the circumstances of the visitor’s travel to Bulgaria and may not exceed five years.
Customer cannot work in Bulgaria on the basis of a short-term visa. 
Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued.
An issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Bulgaria. On every crossing of the border, the police may require the foreign citizen
                                                                        to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits. 
The decision to refuse a visa, indicating reasons for such a decision, shall be delivered to the Customer in a visa refusal/annulment/revocation form.
A foreign citizen can file a complaint against a decision to refuse to issue a visa at any time suitable for him. 
The visa application fee is non-refundable once the application has been accepted at the Embassy.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution

It is issued for one, two, or multiple entries for the purpose of transit, tourism, business, private or other purposes. Its validity period depends on the circumstances of the visitor’s travel to Bulgaria and may not exceed five years.
Customer cannot work in Bulgaria on the basis of a short-term visa. 
Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued.
An issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Bulgaria. On every crossing of the border, the police may require the foreign citizen
                                                                        to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits. 
The decision to refuse a visa, indicating reasons for such a decision, shall be delivered to the Customer in a visa refusal/annulment/revocation form.
A foreign citizen can file a complaint against a decision to refuse to issue a visa at any time suitable for him. 
The visa application fee is non-refundable once the application has been accepted at the Embassy.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution

[Table:Invitation or Private Visit]
Please Note: 
There is a service charge of 17 565 KZT (Inclusive of VAT 16%) levied per application over and above the visa fees
Note: All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable. 
Please note, that all payments for visa and service fee accepted in local currency (KZT).
                                                                    

## Documents Required
The Customers are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
- A filled in visa application (all columns need to be filled in with blue ink). The application shall be filled in completely and legibly in block letters. Names shall mandatorily be spelled in Latin letters (as spelled in the international passport). The rest of the information shall be completed in Russian and English. The application shall be signed by the Customer in person; In the form of minors children, BOTH parents must be subscribe their sign. If there is a signature from only the one parent, they must provide a notarized power of attorney for the right to sign and submit.
- A foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid for at least six months after the end of the intended trip (for a multiple-entry visa – after the end of the last intended trip), containing at least two blank pages.
- A photocopy of the first page of international passport and a photocopy of the latest Bulgarian and Schengen visas if any.
- A 3.5 by 4.5 cm color photograph with a light background. Face one and the face in it shall be clear and large, occupying 70 to 80% of the photograph. No red, eye effect shall be visible. Photographs in which the person wears darkened glasses, art (unprofessional) photographs, or images cropped from amateur photographs WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.
- Travel health insurance to cover the possible expenses related to repatriation, for health reasons, urgent medical assistance and/or urgent hospitalization, with the minimum coverage of EUR 30 000. The insurance must be valid for the territory of Schengen area or WorldWide.
- Invitation letter as per the approved Format from a Bulgarian individual or foreign nationals with Bulgarian legal residency (original and copy); stamped by the Bulgarian Immigration authorities. Proof of relationship (for close relatives, if applying for a multiple visa); i.e. Birth certificate or marriage certificates ONLY
- Employee letter on official letterhead with stamp, address, phone number, position and salary of person travelling. Sponsoring company clearly mentioning the duration and purpose of the trip;
- Proof of the availability of necessary financial resources: bank statement (with the calculation of 60 euros for one day), bank statement (for the last 3 months).
- Invitation letter from a Bulgarian individual (original and copy);
Copy of the identity document of the inviting person.
Proof of relationship (for close relatives, if applying for a multiple-entry visa);
Proof of accommodation during the stay in Bulgaria:
- Notarial deed of purchase of real estate (legible photocopy – seals and signatures must be clearly visible) and notarized consent for residence from the property owner or 
- If the notarial deed states that the property is under construction (Acts 14/15), then in order to obtain a visa it is necessary to present Act 16 for commissioning of the property (original + photocopy), or a document confirming advance payment of a booking in tourist accommodation facilities, or a declaration of invitation from a Bulgarian individual or legal entity, by which the inviting party undertakes to cover all expenses for the period of stay in Bulgaria; 
Means of transport and intention to return to the country of residence:

Reservation of a return ticket by plane or bus; if the person is traveling to a third country, then confirmation of the route (travel ticket, etc.).

Proof of family ties (copies of marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.).

If the client owns real estate in the Republic of Bulgaria:

Owner’s property visa:

Property owners, their spouse, and children under 18 years old (other individuals who provide a Notarial Deed may also obtain a single-entry visa). For this, a notarized power of attorney is required, allowing them to reside in the property for the necessary period.

Original and copy of the Notarial Deed.

Documents confirming that taxes and fees for the previous year have been paid (if applying for a multiple-entry visa); Certificate of commissioning (Act 16) or Act 15, if it is clearly stated in the Notarial Deed that the property is under construction.
- Means of transportation and intention to return to the country of residence: reservation of return travel ticket for airplane, but, If the person is travelling to a third country, then proof of itinerary (travel ticket, etc.).
- Proof of family ties (copies of marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.)
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR TRAVELING WITH MINORS

Submission of a visa application for minors:
- Young children (under 14) or minors under guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Minors (aged 14 to 18) or persons under limited guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the minor and also by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Parental presence, signature, or consent is not required if the parent: 
- has been deprived of parental rights – an apostilled notarized copy of the final court decision must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is deceased – an apostilled notarized copy of the parent’s death certificate must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is absent (if the birth certificate lists as “father” a close person or someone who is not the legal parent) – the original document issued by the competent state authority confirming that the father’s name was entered in the birth certificate based on the mother’s statement must be provided.
- Documents confirming the father’s absence are not required only if the birth certificate shows a dash in the “father” field. In this case, an apostilled notarized copy of the birth certificate must be included in the document set.
- If the child has no parents but has an official guardian, the corresponding document establishing guardianship must be provided. An apostilled notarized copy of the guardianship document with a translation into Bulgarian must be included.
- For minors traveling without their parents/guardians and requesting a single/multiple-entry visa, a notarized copy of the notarized consent/authorization (either for travel with another person or unaccompanied) from both parents/guardians or from the parent/guardian not accompanying the minor must be provided for the entire duration of the requested visa.
- In all cases, a copy of the birth certificate is required for minors, as well as a separate set of visa application documents, even if the minor is included in a parent’s passport.
Applications for minor children must be submitted:
- On the same day together with the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip; or
- Not earlier than the application of the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip is under review at the consulate; or
- If the accompanying adult holds a valid Schengen visa for the entire duration of the trip.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa Customer must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last 6 months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa Customer submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa Customer is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the Customer's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The average visa processing time is 15 calendar days after the application is accepted at the embassy (processing may be faster, depending on the documents). In some cases, if additional consideration is necessary, this period can be extended to a maximum of 45-60 calendar days.

## Download Forms
Dear Customers, please note that all Bulgaria visa application centers offer assisted form filling service. 
To download a document required please choose one of the links below:
Visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3V3eQu0QUCGhOP6jd6Ci7p/2f78c24ee2ae23cf4b8e1f6ed653b811/visa_application_form_latest.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.
Please note:
- Visa application form should be on 3 pages.
- It should be printed both sides.
You can also get preprinted application form in the Visa Application Center in Astana and Almaty.

## Overview
Citizens of Kazakhstan who plan to visit Bulgaria are required to have a visa.
IMPORTANT NOTICE
Citizens of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan don’t need be a residence of Kazakhstan for submitting documents for visa (they do not have an Embassy).
A short-stay visa (C-visa) allows the visitor to transit through or stay in the territory of the Republic of Bulgaria for a maximum of 90 days in any period 180 day-period. It is issued for one, two, or multiple entries for the purpose of transit, tourism, business, private or other purposes. Its validity period depends on the circumstances of the visitor’s travel to Bulgaria and may not exceed five years.
Customers cannot work in Bulgaria by short term visa.
Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued.
An issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Bulgaria. On every crossing of the border, the police may require the foreign citizen to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits.
The decision to refuse a visa, indicating reasons for such a decision, shall be delivered to the Customer in a visa refusal/annulment/revocation form.
A foreign citizen can file a complaint against a decision to refuse to issue a visa at any time suitable for him.
The visa application fee is non-refundable once the application has been accepted at the Embassy.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution.

## Visa Fees

[Table:SHORT-TERM EMPLOYMENT]
Please Note: 
There is a service charge of 17 565 KZT (Inclusive of VAT 16%) levied per application over and above the visa fees
Note: All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable. 
Please note, that all payments for visa and service fee accepted in local currency (KZT).
                                                                    

## Documents Required
The Customers are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
- A filled in visa application (all columns need to be filled in with blue ink). The application shall be filled in completely and legibly in block letters. Names shall mandatorily be spelled in Latin letters (as spelled in the international passport). The rest of the information shall be completed in Russian and English. The application shall be signed by the Customer in person; In the form of minors children, BOTH parents must be subscribe their sign. If there is a signature from only one parent, they must provide a notarized power of attorney for the right to sign and submit.
- A foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid for at least six months after the end of the intended trip (for a multiple-entry visa – after the end of the last intended trip), containing at least two blank pages.
- A photocopy of the first page of his international passport and a photocopy of the latest Bulgarian and Schengen visas.
- A 3.5 by 4.5 cm color photograph with a light background. Face one and the face in it shall be clear and large, occupying 70 to 80% of the photograph. No red, eye effect shall be visible. Photographs in which the person wears darkened glasses, art (unprofessional) photographs, or images cropped from amateur photographs WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.
- Travel health insurance to cover the possible expenses related to repatriation, for health reasons, urgent medical assistance and/or urgent hospitalization, with the minimum coverage of EUR 30 000. The insurance must be valid for the territory of Schengen area or Worldwide.
- Means of transportation and intention to return to the country of residence: reservation of return travel ticket for airplane and by bus. If the person is travelling to a third country, then proof of itinerary (travel ticket, etc.).
- Invitation- Declaration in the prescribed format (photocopy) from the host employer, registered with the “Employment Agency” under the Ministry of Labour and Social Policy of the Republic of Bulgaria. The declaration must include the following information: applicant’s full name, place of work, position, duration of the contract, employer’s address, contact details, signature and stamp of the employer. The Invitation-Declaration is valid for 6 months from the date of certification.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa Customer must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last 6 months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa Customer submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa Customer is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the Customer's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The average visa processing time is 15 calendar days after the application is accepted at the embassy (processing may be faster, depending on the documents). In some cases, if additional consideration is necessary, this period can be extended to a maximum of 45-60 calendar days.

## Download Forms
Dear Customers, please note that all Bulgaria visa application centers offer assisted form filling service. 
To download a document required please choose one of the links below:
Visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3V3eQu0QUCGhOP6jd6Ci7p/2f78c24ee2ae23cf4b8e1f6ed653b811/visa_application_form_latest.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.
Please note:
- Visa application form should be on 3 pages.
- It should be printed both sides.
You can also get preprinted application form in the Visa Application Center in Astana and Almaty.

The application shall be signed by the Customer in person; In the form of minors children, BOTH parents must be subscribe their sign. If there is a signature from only the one parent, they must provide a notarized power of attorney for the right to sign and submit.
- A foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid for at least six months after the end of the intended trip (for a multiple-entry visa – after the end of the last intended trip), containing at least two blank pages.
- A photocopy of the first page of his international passport and a photocopy of the latest Bulgarian and Schengen visas.
- A 3.5 by 4.5 cm color photograph with a light background. Face one and the face in it shall be clear and large, occupying 70 to 80% of the photograph. No red, eye effect shall be visible. Photographs in which the person wears darkened glasses, art (unprofessional) photographs, or images cropped from amateur photographs WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.
- Travel health insurance to cover the possible expenses related to repatriation, for health reasons, urgent medical assistance and/or urgent hospitalization, with the minimum coverage of EUR 30 000. The insurance must be valid for the territory of Schengen area or WorlWide.
- Proof of residence for nationals other than Kazakhstan: a photocopy of the stay permit; Citizens of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan don’t need be a residence of Kazakhstan for submitting documents for visa (they do not have an Embassy).
Citizens of Russian Federation must obtain relevant temporaty residence permit or residence permit card than must be valid 3 months upon returning to Kazakhstan.
- A certificate from the place of employment on official letterhead with a stamp, address, phone number, position, and salary of the traveler. If all expenses are covered by the employer, a business travel letter indicating the purpose and duration of the trip must be provided.
For individual entrepreneurs: copies of the certificate of registration as an individual entrepreneur and the taxpayer identification number. 
- Invitation letter in the standard format from Bulgarian legal entity (Pokana Deklaratsiya) foreign representative office in the standard format (copy) stamped by the Bulgarian Immigration authorities. 
If the host party does not provide accommodation (this must be stated in the Invitation Letter), additional documents confirming accommodation are required (a Booking.com reservation is not accepted). 
The voucher or reservation must be on the hotel’s official letterhead with signature and stamp, listing each traveler, length of stay, and a description of the hotel (phone number, address, and contact person at the hotel). 
- Recommendation letter given by Bulgarian Chamber of Commerce, which proves that they have joint business activity or notarized copy of the contract between the 2 companies, proving long term business partnership and the intentions connected with the forthcoming trips, if applying for multiple entry visa.
- Means of transportation and intention to return to the country of residence: reservation of return travel ticket for airplane and by bus, If the person is travelling to a third country, then proof of itinerary (travel ticket, etc.).
- Proof of family ties (copies of marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.) 

For minor children traveling without parents or guardians: 
- A copy of the child's birth certificate;
- The original and copy of a notarized power of attorney from both parents / guardians or one of the parents / guardians that they give their consent to the child to go abroad. In case they do not accompany a minor child. If one of the parents is not responsible for the child, a certificate (copy) of the deprivation of parental rights from the court must be provided. Also, if one of the parents is registered in the birth certificate in the words of the father / mother, it is necessary to provide the necessary documents confirming this.
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR TRAVELING WITH MINORS

Submission of a visa application for minors:
- Young children (under 14) or minors under guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Minors (aged 14 to 18) or persons under limited guardianship: the visa application must be signed by the minor and also by the legal representatives (full names and signatures of both parents are required), or by one parent if they have a notarized power of attorney from the other parent, or by an authorized person (original notarized power of attorney from both parents).
- Parental presence, signature, or consent is not required if the parent: 
- has been deprived of parental rights – an apostilled notarized copy of the final court decision must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is deceased – an apostilled notarized copy of the parent’s death certificate must be provided, along with a translation into Bulgarian;
- is absent (if the birth certificate lists as “father” a close person or someone who is not the legal parent) – the original document issued by the competent state authority confirming that the father’s name was entered in the birth certificate based on the mother’s statement must be provided.
- Documents confirming the father’s absence are not required only if the birth certificate shows a dash in the “father” field. In this case, an apostilled notarized copy of the birth certificate must be included in the document set.
- If the child has no parents but has an official guardian, the corresponding document establishing guardianship must be provided. An apostilled notarized copy of the guardianship document with a translation into Bulgarian must be included.
- For minors traveling without their parents/guardians and requesting a single/multiple-entry visa, a notarized copy of the notarized consent/authorization (either for travel with another person or unaccompanied) from both parents/guardians or from the parent/guardian not accompanying the minor must be provided for the entire duration of the requested visa.
- In all cases, a copy of the birth certificate is required for minors, as well as a separate set of visa application documents, even if the minor is included in a parent’s passport.
Applications for minor children must be submitted:
- On the same day together with the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip; or
- Not earlier than the application of the accompanying adult for the entire duration of the trip is under review at the consulate; or
- If the accompanying adult holds a valid Schengen visa for the entire duration of the trip.
